prompt: Triage the OrderService price-validation incident using the attached error log, the affected-orders JSON, and the postmortem. Complete the numbered subtask you are given.

documents.title: OrderService error log (batch B-77)
documents.content:
2026-06-20T10:00:01Z INFO  OrderService starting batch B-77
2026-06-20T10:00:02Z INFO  OrderService loaded 1200 orders
2026-06-20T10:00:03Z ERROR OrderService OrderValidationError sku=X-9931 err_code=PRICE_MISSING order=O-5001
2026-06-20T10:00:03Z WARN  OrderService slow catalog lookup 800ms
2026-06-20T10:00:04Z ERROR OrderService OrderValidationError sku=X-8820 err_code=PRICE_MISSING order=O-5002
2026-06-20T10:00:05Z ERROR OrderService OrderValidationError sku=X-9931 err_code=PRICE_MISSING order=O-5003
2026-06-20T10:00:06Z INFO  OrderService batch B-77 aborted after 3 rejections

documents.title: Postmortem O-INC-88 (summary)
documents.content:
# Postmortem O-INC-88 (summary)
Batch B-77 aborted after a burst of orders failed price validation.
Investigation found that the price-catalog sync job, `catalog-sync`, had
silently skipped SKUs created in the preceding 60 minutes due to a cursor bug,
so those newly-added SKUs had no price row. OrderService then rejected any order
referencing those SKUs with err_code=PRICE_MISSING. The downstream OrderService
and the WarehouseService were NOT at fault; the trigger was entirely upstream in
catalog-sync. Fix: patch the catalog-sync cursor and backfill missing prices.

subtasks.prompt: From the OrderService error log, the ERROR lines share a constant error class and err_code. Output the signature EXACTLY as `<ErrorClass>|<err_code>` taken from the first ERROR line. Put only that between <answer> and </answer> tags.
In the events JSON, count the number of DISTINCT users whose status is exactly "failed". Output only that integer between <answer> and </answer> tags.
Per the postmortem, which single upstream job is the root cause of the incident? Output its exact name as written in the document, between <answer> and </answer> tags.

Write a Python function `retry_delay(attempt)` giving the backoff delay in seconds for a 0-indexed attempt: delay = 2 ** attempt, but never more than a cap of 30. Examples: retry_delay(0)==1, retry_delay(1)==2, retry_delay(3)==8. Respond with a SINGLE ```python code block defining retry_delay and nothing else.

Produce a one-line status update EXACTLY matching this template, substituting the given values (keep the literal spacing and separators):
  [RESOLVED] <service> | sig=<signature> | users=<n> | cause=<job>
with service=OrderService, signature=OrderValidationError|PRICE_MISSING, n=3, job=catalog-sync. Put only the resulting line between <answer> and </answer> tags.
